payload.goal: Surface note_added events on the resume read surfaces — get_state digest, SessionStart status block, sofar status — so a recorded correction can reach the next session instead of dying invisible in the log. Driver: the 2026-07-11 incident — '0.5.0 published' was recorded as a note minutes after the write-back, but no surface renders notes, so the stale next_action won and a session re-proposed done work. Staleness detection (0.4.0) announces THAT the record drifted; this initiative shows WHAT changed. Constraint: compaction-proofing discipline — count-capped, budget-boxed, one-line entries that never crowd out rationale (decisions + rejected-approaches ledger).

payload.cmd: git checkout -b notes-in-digest && sofar new notes-in-digest --goal "Surface note_added events on the resume read surfaces — get_state digest, SessionStart status block, sofar status — so a recorded correction can reach the next session instead of dying invisible in the log. Driver: the 2026-07-11 incident — '0.5.0 published' was recorded as a note minutes after the write-back, but no surface renders notes, so the stale next_action won and a session re-proposed done work. Staleness detection (0.4.0) announces THAT the record drifted; this initiative shows WHAT changed. Constraint: compaction-proofing discipline — count-capped, budget-boxed, one-line entries that never crowd out rationale (decisions + rejected-approaches ledger)."

tasks.title: Pre-register the note-selection rule as a logged decision BEFORE building: which notes render — notes since last write-back (the correction window, pairs with staleness), last-K overall, or both with separate caps; include why the losing options lose
Fold: expose the selected notes on state (ts + text, ordered), zero new event types, deterministic replay (same log → deep-equal state)

renderStatus (SessionStart block + get_state digest via the shared seam): budgeted notes section — new per-section budget const, count cap, one-line clipped entries; rendered adjacent to the staleness warning so drift-signal and drift-content read together
renderFullStatus (sofar status): uncapped notes section on the terminal surface
SPEC §State (derivation shape) + §MCP tools/§Hooks (surface behavior); §Acceptance: notes render when present and are absent when none; 10k SessionStart cap holds with all sections at worst case
Fold tests: selection rule over synthetic logs (incl. notes straddling the write-back boundary), replay determinism
Render tests: budget + count cap respected, clip behavior, absent-when-none, enforceStatusLimit ≤10k at worst case; digest and SessionStart parity via the shared seam

payload.cmd: git add .sofar && git commit -m "record: new initiative notes-in-digest — surface note_added on resume read surfaces (seeded plan)" && git push -u origin notes-in-digest && sofar status | head -22

sofar status --help; echo "==="; grep -n "initiatives\|readdir\|--all" /Users/jins/IO/sofar/packages/engine/src/cli/status.ts 2>/dev/null | head; ls /Users/jins/IO/sofar/packages/engine/src/cli/ | head -20

grep -n "slug\|binding" /Users/jins/IO/sofar/packages/engine/src/cli/status.ts | head -8; echo "=== serve /state ==="; grep -n "'/state'\|allInitiatives\|listInitiatives\|readdirSync" /Users/jins/IO/sofar/packages/engine/src/cli/serve.ts | head -8

payload.chose: Notes selection rule (pre-registered, 1.1): surface notes appended AFTER the last session_ended (the correction window), or ALL notes when nothing ever wrote back; carried on state.freshness as {ts, text}[] in log order, uncapped at fold; renderStatus count-caps + budget-clips, renderFullStatus renders the selection uncapped
payload.over: (a) last-K notes overall regardless of write-back boundary; (b) both windows with separate caps
payload.because: Since-write-back IS the incident shape: every selected note postdates the summary/next_action the reader is about to trust, so each is un-absorbed by construction; it shares the exact reset event (session_ended) and lifecycle with freshness counters — staleness says THAT the record drifted, this shows WHAT — and it is self-clearing, so the section cannot accumulate (compaction-proof by construction, not cap tuning). Last-K loses: no natural clearing — an absorbed note re-renders forever, spending digest budget on stale content, and K misfires both ways (K absorbed notes render while the K+1th unabsorbed correction vanishes); it also makes notes a competing durable-memory channel with repo.md/decisions. Both-with-caps loses: two note sections double budget spend and force the reader to reconcile overlapping lists; old notes remain reachable via get_state view:full and the log. Accepted edge (consistent with freshness counter semantics): a note landing before an in-flight write-back counts as absorbed even if the ending agent never saw it — same boundary rule as the staleness counters, and the next SessionStart re-orients anyway.
